title: A craft that is disappearing in my town
summary: Explain one old craft or skill that fewer people do now, and describe the person who still keeps it alive.
topic: An old craft or hand-skill that is slowly disappearing
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a traditional craft (for example basket making, shoe repair, hand weaving, key cutting). Say what the craft is, who still does it, and why fewer young people learn it now. Use present simple and past simple, and the linkers 'and', 'but', 'because'.
hintStarters: In my town there is an old man who makes…
He learned this craft from… many years ago, but now…
I think this craft is disappearing because…
sampleText: In my town there is an old man named Mr. Tan. He makes baskets by hand. His small workshop is behind the market. I often stop and watch him work. He uses thin pieces of bamboo and his fingers move slowly. He learned this craft from his father many years ago. He says a good basket takes three days. People in my town used these baskets every day in the past. They carried fruit, fish, and rice in them. But now most people buy plastic bags because they are cheaper and faster. Young people do not want to learn this craft. It is hard and the money is small. Mr. Tan is patient, but he is sad. He has no student now. I think this craft is disappearing because machines make things faster. Last month I bought one basket from him. It is strong and beautiful. I use it for my vegetables. When I look at it, I think about his hands. I hope someone learns his skill before it is too late.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He learned this craft from his father
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông học nghề này từ cha của mình
chúng rẻ hơn và nhanh hơn
nghề thủ công này đang biến mất

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Last month I bought one basket
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tháng trước tôi đã mua một cái giỏ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'because' to give a reason
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'because' để nêu lý do, theo sau là một mệnh đề đầy đủ: 'because they are cheaper and faster', 'because machines make things faster'. Đây là cách đơn giản và đúng để giải thích nguyên nhân ở trình độ A1.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'because' to give a reason, followed by a full clause: 'because they are cheaper and faster' and 'because machines make things faster'. This is the simplest correct way to explain a cause at A1.

sampleTranslationVi: Ở thị trấn của tôi có một ông lão tên là ông Tân. Ông làm giỏ bằng tay. Xưởng nhỏ của ông nằm phía sau khu chợ. Tôi thường dừng lại và xem ông làm việc. Ông dùng những thanh tre mỏng và những ngón tay của ông di chuyển chậm rãi. Ông học nghề này từ cha của mình nhiều năm trước. Ông nói một chiếc giỏ tốt mất ba ngày. Người dân trong thị trấn ngày xưa dùng những chiếc giỏ này mỗi ngày. Họ đựng trái cây, cá và gạo trong đó. Nhưng bây giờ hầu hết mọi người mua túi nhựa vì chúng rẻ hơn và nhanh hơn. Người trẻ không muốn học nghề này. Nó vất vả và tiền thì ít. Ông Tân kiên nhẫn, nhưng ông buồn. Bây giờ ông không có học trò. Tôi nghĩ nghề này đang biến mất vì máy móc làm mọi thứ nhanh hơn. Tháng trước tôi đã mua một cái giỏ từ ông. Nó chắc chắn và đẹp. Tôi dùng nó để đựng rau. Khi nhìn nó, tôi nghĩ về đôi bàn tay của ông. Tôi hy vọng có ai đó học được kỹ năng của ông trước khi quá muộn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'He ___ baskets by hand.'
options: make
making
maked
makes
mcQuestions.hint: The subject is 'he' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple to say where he learned the craft?
options: He learned this craft from his father.
He learn this craft from his father.
He learned this craft from his father yesterday many years ago.
He is learning this craft from his father years ago.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'learned' is the correct past simple; the other options mix tenses or contradict the time.
mcQuestions.hint: Which sentence has one clear past-simple verb and no time conflict?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why people buy plastic bags now?
options: People buy plastic bags because cheaper faster.
People buy plastic bags because they are cheaper and faster.
People buy plastic bags so cheap and fast they.
Because cheaper and faster people plastic bags buy.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by a complete clause: 'they are cheaper and faster'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' correctly to show a contrast?
options: Mr. Tan is patient, but sad he is is.
Mr. Tan patient is, but he sad.
Mr. Tan is patient, but he is sad.
But Mr. Tan is patient he is sad.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two full clauses, each with subject + 'is' + adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: Both clauses after 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'Machines ___ things faster.'
options: makes
making
is make
make

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says young people do not want to learn?
options: Young people do not want to learn this craft.
Young people does not want to learn this craft.
Young people not want learn this craft.
Young people do not wants to learning this craft.
mcQuestions.explanation: Plural subject uses 'do not' + base verb 'want to learn'.
mcQuestions.hint: With a plural subject, is it 'do not' or 'does not'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for last month?
options: Last month I buyed one basket from him.
Last month I bought one basket from him.
Last month I buy one basket from him.
Last month I have bought one basket from him.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'bought' is the irregular past simple of 'buy'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds the most natural and on-topic?
options: I often stop and watch him to work.
Often I stop and watching him work.
I often stop and watch him work.
I often stop and watch him download the work.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'watch him work' (object + bare infinitive) is correct, and 'often' goes before the main verb.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'watch him', the next verb has no 'to'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes the craft today?
options: I think this craft is disappear.
I think this craft are disappearing.
I think this craft is disappeared every day.
I think this craft is disappearing.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'is disappearing' (present continuous for a slow change) with singular 'craft'.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'is' + verb-ing for a change happening now.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural way to end with a hope?
options: I hope someone learns his skill before it is too late.
I hope someone learn his skill before it is too late.
I hope someone learns his skill before it is too lately.
I hope someone learns his skill before it is too late machine.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'someone learns' (singular -s) and 'too late' are both correct and natural.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb after 'someone' and the phrase 'too late'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If a craft disappears completely, what do you think a town loses besides the objects the craft made?
